pr.title: Remove bss completely, fix a fakematch on id.c from a mistake in logic
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Deleted the monolithic asm/bss.s object from the build and redistributed .bss reservations into the owning assembly or C translation units. The PR also corrected a sysdolphin baselib id.c fake match by changing HSD_IDTable from an array of IDEntry structs to an array of IDEntry pointers, replacing partial 0x194 memsets with sizeof(HSD_IDTable), and promoting several lbl_* globals to named C definitions such as hsd_iddata and default_table.
what_changed: Removed $(BSS_O_FILES) from the main object list in Makefile.
Removed the BSS_O_FILES definition pointing at $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/bss.o from obj_files.mk.
Deleted asm/bss.s, which had contained 1239 lines of global .bss labels and .skip reservations.

Added .section .bss, "wa" blocks to many owning assembly files, including MSL/abort_exit.s, MetroTRK/nubinit.s, Runtime/NMWException.s, dolphin SDK files, melee module asm files, and sysdolphin/baselib asm files.

Changed several baselib globals from extern lbl_* declarations to actual named C definitions, including HSD_ObjAllocData aobj_alloc_data, fobj_alloc_data, zlist_alloc_data, hsd_iddata, robj_alloc_data, and rvalue_alloc_data.

Renamed documented global variables in include/variables.h from address-style lbl_* names to semantic names such as FrameBuffer, memReport, rmode, iparam_fifo_size, DefaultFifoObj, init_done, black, hsd_iddata, default_table, and endcallback_list.
In src/sysdolphin/baselib/id.h, changed HSD_IDTable.table from struct _IDEntry table[101] to struct _IDEntry* table[101].
In src/sysdolphin/baselib/id.c, replaced the old lbl_804C23C0 and lbl_804C23EC externs with HSD_ObjAllocData hsd_iddata and HSD_IDTable default_table.
In id.c, changed HSD_IDSetup and _HSD_IDForgetMemory from memset(..., 0, 0x194) on a larger fake struct to memset(..., 0, sizeof(HSD_IDTable)) on the corrected pointer-table struct.
Updated inline assembly symbol references in id.c and initialize.c to use semantic global names while preserving the same absolute addresses encoded in comments.
smart_moves: Removing the central bss.o object forced BSS ownership to live with the object that actually owns each symbol, which is more compatible with incremental decompilation than a single catch-all bss.s file.
The id.c fix recognized that the previous struct layout only matched because the code partially zeroed 0x194 bytes; modeling the table as 101 pointers explains the memset size naturally.
Using sizeof(HSD_IDTable) instead of a magic 0x194 documents the intended data layout and makes future struct edits safer.
Promoting unnamed lbl_* storage to named C definitions lets matched or partially matched source files own their BSS directly instead of depending on external assembly reservations.
The PR updated both C references and inline asm relocation names together, avoiding symbol drift between decompiled code and hand-written assembly.
decomp_lessons: A matching-looking memset size can reveal a wrong struct model; if a partial memset seems suspicious, re-check whether the field is actually a pointer array or smaller table rather than forcing the fake layout.
When eliminating a monolithic BSS file, preserve object order by moving each .bss reservation into the assembly or C translation unit that corresponds to its original object.
Replacing address labels with semantic globals is safest when done simultaneously in source, headers or variables documentation, and inline asm symbol operands.
Prefer named C global definitions for decompiled translation units once ownership is understood; leave .skip reservations in assembly only for still-undecompiled objects.
Use sizeof on corrected structs after validating layout, rather than carrying over raw byte counts that may hide fake matches.
naming_conventions: HSD object allocator globals use lowercase subsystem names with _alloc_data, e.g. aobj_alloc_data, fobj_alloc_data, robj_alloc_data, rvalue_alloc_data, zlist_alloc_data.
The baselib ID allocator global is named hsd_iddata.
The default ID table global is named default_table.
Video and initialization globals use existing SDK-style or descriptive names: FrameBuffer, memReport, rmode, iparam_fifo_size, DefaultFifoObj, init_done, black.
Callback list storage was renamed from lbl_804D7628 to endcallback_list.
Unknown globals that still lack semantics remain as lbl_80xxxxxx labels in assembly .bss blocks.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted asm/bss.s and removed bss.o from build inputs instead of leaving an empty central BSS object.
Moved BSS reservations into per-object assembly files using .section .bss, "wa", .global labels, and .skip sizes.
Kept inline assembly in id.c for matching functions, but changed relocation operands from lbl_804C23EC/lbl_804C23C0 to default_table/hsd_iddata.
Corrected HSD_IDTable to an array of IDEntry pointers so sizeof(HSD_IDTable) equals the observed 0x194 bytes.
Adjusted NON_MATCHING C in id.c to use pointer-table indexing such as table->table[hash(id)] instead of address arithmetic over embedded IDEntry structs.
Defined C-owned BSS globals directly by removing extern from declarations where the source file now owns the storage.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the PR slice, so there is no human review feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue moving .bss symbols from assembly into C-owned globals.
Search for HSD_IDTable users to confirm all code treats table entries as IDEntry* rather than embedded IDEntry objects.
Audit remaining memset calls with magic sizes near struct initialization for possible fake matches.
Search variables.h for remaining lbl_804C23* or lbl_804D76* names to identify remaining baselib global naming work.
Check whether id.c NON_MATCHING insertion logic was later cleaned up or fully matched in C.
